# Work Packages

## Requirements Analysis and Reference Study

- Extract and refine the digital requirements from the AES kick-off document and discussions.

- Study the LED1642GW in detail to understand its digital functions, PWM behaviour, interface structure, and limitations.

- Identify constraints coming from the SG13G2 technology such as clock restrictions, IO conditions, and available supply domains.

## Behavioural Modelling (Matlab/Simulink + HDL Coder)

- Develop a high-level model that describes the expected digital behaviour of the driver.

- Use Simulink and HDL Coder to verify functional correctness before writing RTL code.

- Compare model behaviour against the expected reference operation.

## Digital Micro-Architecture in SystemVerilog

- Translate the behavioural model and requirements into a synthesizable RTL design.

- Follow Posy’s coding style and general digital design best practices.

## FPGA Prototyping and Experimental Validation

- Use cocotb to create a testbench and verify the RTL design in simulation.

- Prototype the design on a GateMate FPGA and validate behaviour under real hardware conditions.

## Documentation

- Write the complete thesis manuscript in Quarto, following the Thesis Style Guide of Hochschule Bremen.

- Include diagrams, results, verification data, and clear explanations of all design decisions.
